# Prole

> Prole is a free, native macOS menu-bar app that captures any region of your screen, lets you mark it up and type a question, then copies a single composite image you paste into Claude. It is a visual "ask about my screen" tool — pixels and your note only, no DOM or code extraction.

Prole runs on macOS 12 or later (universal — Apple Silicon and Intel). It is ad-hoc signed, so first launch needs a right-click → Open. Download: https://prole.jeffrey.build/download/Prole.dmg

## How it works

1. Trigger a snip from the menu bar, a floating button, or a global hotkey, then draw a rectangle around any region of any app.
2. Mark it up with rectangles, arrows, freehand pen, and text labels, then type a "Message to Claude".
3. Hit Copy — the annotated image and your note land on the clipboard together. Paste into the Claude desktop or web app and ask.

## Key facts

- Price: free
- Platform: macOS 12+ (universal: Apple Silicon and Intel)
- Distribution: ad-hoc signed .dmg (right-click → Open on first launch; grant Screen Recording permission on first capture)
- What it captures: screen pixels plus your typed note (no source code, selectors, or DOM)
- Made for pasting into: the Claude desktop or web app

## Links

- Home: https://prole.jeffrey.build/
- Download (macOS .dmg): https://prole.jeffrey.build/download/Prole.dmg
